question: been running a small tilapia farm for restaurant supply, current setup gets about 40 fish per cubic meter. consultant says i could push it to 65-70 per meter and double my output with minimal equipment changes, just need better filtration. margins are thin right now and restaurant demand is way up. is this worth the investment or should i jsut expand the pond area instead

ok so our university dining wants to add live cooking stations where students can watch chefs prepare fresh lobster and crab. its trendy and would drive meal plan sales, but kitchens worried about storage logistics and prep timing since we'd need to keep them alive until service. what setup would actually work for this

organizing a cultural festival with traditional live fish preparation demonstrations. vendor offers two options - tank setup where fish are dispatched immediately before cooking, or pre-killed fish that looks less authentic but easier to handle. community expects the real experience and sponsors are pushing for authenticity. which direction makes sense logistically

we're switching to a welfare-certified egg supplier for our hospital cafeteria after some bad press about our food sourcing. new supplier is 30% more expensive and claims cage-free but their facility tour keeps getting postponed. administration wants this resolved quickly to show we're responsive. should we just announce the switch now or wait to verify their setup first
